title: Middletonia regularis
taxonomy: Middletonia regularis is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Proteaceae. It is native to New Zealand and is one of the few remaining species in the Middletonia genus. The classification of Middletonia regularis places it within the kingdom Plantae, with its more specific classification being a member of the subfamily Proteoideae. The plant's classification is further defined by its order (Proteales), class (Magnoliopsida), and division (Angiospermae). intro: Middletonia regularis, also known as Middletonia, is a plant species native to New Zealand and Australia, characterized by its unique, yellow-petaled flowers. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its use in traditional medicine to alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia. Middletonia contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is traditionally used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
